outline.title: Async Python for Backend Engineers
outline.overview: In this four-week course, backend engineers familiar with synchronous Django or Flask will learn the fundamentals of asynchronous programming in Python. The course covers the event loop, asyncio primitives, async ORMs, and testing async code, providing a comprehensive introduction to building high-performance async applications. By the end of the course, learners will be able to design and implement efficient async systems, leveraging the power of async/await and asyncio.

lessons.title: Introduction to the Event Loop in Python
Understanding Async/Await Syntax in Python 3.13
Running Async Code with asyncio.run()
Module 1 Quiz: Event Loop and Async Basics
Working with asyncio.gather() and asyncio.wait()
Using asyncio.create_task() for Concurrent Execution
Asyncio Locks and Semaphores for Synchronization
Module 2 Quiz: Asyncio Primitives and Concurrency
Introduction to Async ORMs like SQLAlchemy and Django ORM
Using async/await with Database Queries
Best Practices for Async Database Transactions
Module 3 Quiz: Async ORMs and Database Integration
Testing Async Functions with unittest and pytest
Debugging Async Code with PDB and print Statements
Using asyncio.test_utils for Async Testing
Module 4 Quiz: Testing and Debugging Async Code

judge.rationale: outline covers required topics but lacks depth in some areas

outline.title: Building Production-Ready FastAPI Services
outline.overview: This self-paced course is designed for mid-level Python engineers looking to ship their first FastAPI service. It covers the essential concepts and best practices for building production-ready FastAPI applications, including project layout, dependency injection, security middleware, observability, and deploying with Docker. By the end of this course, you will have the knowledge and skills to design, develop, and deploy scalable and secure FastAPI services. The course is structured into four modules, each focusing on a critical aspect of building production-ready FastAPI applications.

lessons.title: Best Practices for Organizing FastAPI Projects
Using Poetry for Dependency Management in FastAPI
Project Layout and Dependency Management Quiz
Introduction to Dependency Injection in FastAPI
Implementing Security Middleware in FastAPI
Dependency Injection and Security Middleware Quiz
Logging Best Practices in FastAPI Applications
Monitoring and Observability with OpenTelemetry in FastAPI
Observability and Logging in FastAPI Quiz
Introduction to Containerization with Docker
Building and Deploying FastAPI Services with Docker
Deploying FastAPI Services with Docker Quiz

judge.rationale: The outline covers most required topics but lacks depth in some areas.

outline.title: Practical Model Evaluation in Machine Learning
outline.overview: This course is designed for data scientists who are familiar with the basics of scikit-learn and want to dive deeper into the practical aspects of model evaluation. It covers key concepts such as cross-validation, calibration, and error analysis, and provides guidance on how to ship a model with confidence. By the end of this course, learners will be able to evaluate and improve their machine learning models, and deploy them in real-world applications. The course is divided into four modules, each focusing on a critical aspect of model evaluation, with a mix of theoretical foundations and practical examples.

lessons.title: Introduction to Cross-Validation Techniques
Understanding Overfitting and Underfitting in Machine Learning
Model Evaluation Metrics: Precision, Recall, and F1 Score
Module 1 Quiz: Model Evaluation Basics
Introduction to Calibration in Machine Learning Models
Methods for Calibration: Platt Scaling and Isotonic Regression
Uncertainty Estimation: Bayesian Neural Networks and Monte Carlo Dropout
Module 2 Quiz: Calibration and Uncertainty
Error Analysis: Identifying and Addressing Model Biases
Model Interpretability: Feature Importance and Partial Dependence Plots
Techniques for Model Improvement: Hyperparameter Tuning and Model Selection
Module 3 Quiz: Error Analysis and Model Improvement
Model Deployment Strategies: Online and Offline Deployment
Model Monitoring and Maintenance: Data Drift and Concept Drift
Best Practices for Model Documentation and Versioning
Module 4 Quiz: Shipping a Model with Confidence

judge.rationale: outline covers key topics but lacks depth in some areas

outline.title: Practical Retrieval-Augmented Generation
outline.overview: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) is a powerful technique that enables software engineers to build more efficient and effective natural language processing systems. This course provides a hands-on introduction to RAG, covering the key concepts of chunking, embeddings, vector search, prompt design, and evaluation. By the end of this course, learners will be able to build a small but real RAG application, and will have a solid foundation for further exploration of this exciting field. Throughout the course, learners will work with practical examples and exercises to reinforce their understanding of RAG concepts and techniques.

lessons.title: Introduction to Retrieval-Augmented Generation and its Applications
Chunking and Text Preprocessing for RAG
Understanding Embeddings and Vector Representations
RAG Foundations Quiz
Vector Search Fundamentals and Algorithms
Indexing and Querying Techniques for Efficient Vector Search
Evaluating Vector Search Systems for RAG
Vector Search and Information Retrieval Quiz
Introduction to Prompt Design for RAG Systems
Prompt Engineering and Optimization Techniques
Evaluating and Refining Prompts for RAG Applications
Prompt Design and Optimization Quiz
Architecture and Design Considerations for RAG Systems
Implementing a Small RAG Application using Popular Libraries
Deploying and Evaluating a RAG Application
Building a RAG Application Quiz

outline.title: Optimizing Postgres for Backend Engineers
outline.overview: Through a combination of lectures, examples, and hands-on exercises, you'll gain a deeper understanding of Postgres internals and learn how to apply this knowledge to real-world problems. Our goal is to help you become a more effective and efficient backend engineer, capable of delivering high-performance applications and services.

lessons.title: Introduction to Indexing in Postgres
Choosing the Right Index Type: B-Tree, Hash, and GiST
Indexing Strategies for Common Query Patterns
Indexing and Query Optimization Quiz
Understanding the EXPLAIN Command and Its Output
Using EXPLAIN to Identify Performance Bottlenecks
Advanced EXPLAIN Techniques: Analyzing Joins and Subqueries
EXPLAIN and Query Optimization Quiz
Introduction to Transactions in Postgres: ACID Properties and Isolation Levels
Managing Transactions: Commit, Rollback, and Savepoints
Concurrency Control and Locking Mechanisms in Postgres
Transaction Management and Concurrency Quiz

modules.title: Production Database Configuration and Best Practices

lessons.title: Configuring Postgres for Production: Memory, CPU, and Disk Settings
Setting Up Database Connections and Authentication
Monitoring and Maintenance: Logging, Statistics, and Backup Strategies

outline.title: Building Accessible UI with React, TypeScript, and Tailwind
outline.overview: Throughout this course, we'll explore topics such as semantic HTML, ARIA attributes, high contrast mode, and keyboard navigation, and learn how to test and validate accessibility using tools like Lighthouse and axe. Whether you're looking to improve the accessibility of an existing application or build a new one from scratch, this course will provide you with the guidance and expertise you need to succeed.

lessons.title: Introduction to WCAG 2.2 AA Guidelines and Accessibility Principles
Semantic HTML and ARIA Attributes in React Applications
Setting Up Accessibility Testing Tools like Lighthouse and axe
Module 1 Quiz: Accessible Foundations
Building Accessible Buttons and Form Controls with React and Tailwind
Creating Accessible Navigation and Menu Systems
Implementing Accessible Modals and Dialogs
Module 2 Quiz: Accessible Components
Designing for High Contrast Mode and Color Accessibility
Creating Accessible Grids and Flexbox Layouts with Tailwind
Implementing Accessible Typography and Font Sizing
Module 3 Quiz: Accessible Layouts and Visual Design

modules.title: Advanced Accessibility Topics and Best Practices

lessons.title: Implementing Accessible Keyboard Navigation and Focus Management
Using TypeScript to Improve Accessibility in React Applications
Best Practices for Testing and Validating Accessibility
Module 4 Quiz: Advanced Accessibility Topics and Best Practices

judge.rationale: The outline covers most required topics but lacks depth in some areas like forms and errors.

outline.title: Building and Operating a Celery + Redis Worker Fleet
outline.overview: The course is divided into four modules, each focusing on a critical aspect of building and operating a Celery + Redis worker fleet. You'll start by learning about task design and implementation, then move on to retries and queues, followed by monitoring and troubleshooting. The final module covers failure modes and how to mitigate them, ensuring that your worker fleet is always running smoothly and efficiently.

lessons.title: Introduction to Celery Task Design
Implementing Tasks with Celery and Redis
Task Serialization and Deserialization
Task Design Best Practices
Module 1 Quiz: Task Design and Implementation
Understanding Celery Retries and Timeouts
Implementing Retries with Celery and Redis
Queue Management with Celery and Redis
Queue Prioritization and Routing
Module 2 Quiz: Retries and Queues
Introduction to Celery Monitoring Tools
Monitoring Celery Worker Performance
Troubleshooting Common Celery Issues
Using Celery Flower for Monitoring and Management
Module 3 Quiz: Monitoring and Troubleshooting
Common Failure Modes in Celery Worker Fleets
Mitigating Worker Failures with Queue Management
Handling Redis Failures and Data Loss
Implementing High Availability with Celery and Redis
Module 4 Quiz: Failure Modes and Mitigation

outline.title: Practical TypeScript for JavaScript Developers
outline.overview: Through a combination of theoretical foundations and practical examples, you'll gain a deep understanding of how TypeScript can help you write more maintainable, efficient, and error-free code. By the end of this course, you'll be equipped to integrate TypeScript into your existing JavaScript workflow, taking your skills to the next level and staying ahead of the curve in the ever-evolving world of web development.

outline.title: Foundations of Agentic AI for Engineers
outline.overview: Throughout the course, learners will explore the fundamentals of agentic AI, including the different types of agents, their characteristics, and the challenges of building agent-shaped systems. The course will also cover the latest developments in the field, including the use of machine learning and other advanced techniques to improve the performance and efficiency of agentic AI systems.

outline.title: Mastering Docker and Docker-Compose for Application Developers
outline.overview: The course covers key concepts such as Docker image layers, networking, and volumes, as well as how to use docker-compose to manage multiple containers and create a seamless local development experience. You will learn through a combination of text-based lessons and quizzes, with plenty of opportunities to practice what you have learned. By the end of the course, you will be able to confidently use Docker and docker-compose to improve your development workflow and take your skills to the next level.

lessons.title: Introduction to Docker and Containerization
Understanding Docker Images and Layers
Working with Docker Containers
Docker Fundamentals Quiz
Docker Networking Basics
Understanding Docker Volumes and Persistent Data
Using Docker Volumes in Development and Production
Docker Networking and Volumes Quiz
Introduction to Docker-Compose and Multi-Container Applications
Creating a Sane Local Development Loop with Docker-Compose
Using Docker-Compose to Manage Multiple Containers
Docker-Compose and Local Development Quiz
Optimizing Docker Images for Size and Performance
Using Docker in CI/CD Pipelines and Automated Testing
Docker Security and Troubleshooting Best Practices
Advanced Topics and Best Practices Quiz
